title: 10 best herbal decoctions for asthma
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for asthma are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their medicinal properties into the liquid. Examples of herbs that help with asthma include thyme, eucalyptus, and marshmallow root. Drinking these decoctions can help to relax airway muscles, reduce inflammation, and thin mucus, making it easier to breathe. This natural remedy has improved lives by providing relief from constant medication and hospital visits, allowing individuals with asthma to live a more active and comfortable lifestyle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with asthma because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds such as triterpenoids and flavonoids. These bioactive molecules have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are key players in the development of asthma symptoms. Additionally, the decoctions' expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. As a result, regular consumption of Kansas coneflower decoctions may help to alleviate asthma symptoms and improve respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Decoctions of Kansas Coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways, a major contributor to asthma symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The decoction's antioxidant properties may help protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas Coneflower has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, including the ability to increase production of interleukin-12 (IL-12), a cytokine that helps regulate immune responses. This may help reduce inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with asthma by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce airway inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with asthma.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia decoctions may help with asthma by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, which can help in managing asthma symptoms.

Alkaloids (Cichoric acid and others): The alkaloids in Echinacea angustifolia decoctions may help with asthma by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the airways from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used because they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Flowers: They are used due to their content of essential oils and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Clean the roots thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop 2-4 grams of the cleaned roots into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Simmer the chopped roots in 1 liter of water for 5-7 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with asthma because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's flavonoids, isoflavones, and triterpenoid saponins have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. Additionally, licorice root has a bronchodilatory effect, which helps to relax the airway muscles and increase airflow. By reducing swelling and opening up the airways, herbal licorice decoctions provide natural relief from asthma symptoms, allowing individuals to manage their condition more effectively.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.

Flavonoids: Licorice decoctions contain flavonoids like luteolin and isoliquiritigenin, which have bronchodilatory effects, meaning they can help relax and widen airway passages, improving lung function in people with asthma.

Phytosterols: Licorice root also contains phytosterols, such as beta-sitosterol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce mucus production and ease respiratory symptoms associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and mucus production in the airways, thereby improving breathing and reducing asthma symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, liquiritin and isoliquiritin): Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the airways from oxidative stress and inflammation, ultimately reducing asthma symptoms and improving lung function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and open airways in asthma patients.
Barks: They are used due to their rich content of glycosides and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and relax bronchial muscles to relieve asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall season.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes or longer.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asparagus decoctions helps with asthma because it contains antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe. The decoction also increases nitric oxide production, which relaxes airway muscles and improves lung function. Additionally, asparagus has been shown to reduce bronchial spasms and improve overall respiratory health, providing relief from asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Asparagus contains high levels of antioxidants such as vitamin C, vitamin E, and beta-carotene, which help to neutralize free radicals in the body and reduce oxidative stress that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Inflammation-reducing flavonoids: Asparagus decoctions contain flavonoid compounds like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating asthma symptoms.

Bronchodilatory saponins: Asparagus contains saponin compounds like asparagine and asparagusic acid, which can help to relax bronchial smooth muscle, dilate airway passages, and improve lung function, providing relief from bronchospasm and wheezing associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Asparagus officinalis have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid in Asparagus officinalis, acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways.
Asparagine: Asparagine, an amino acid found in Asparagus officinalis, has bronchodilatory properties that help relax the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe for individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain asparagine, a compound with expectorant properties that help relieve asthma symptoms.
Leaves: They are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate asthma attacks.
Stems: They are used for their flavonoid content, which can help relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation in asthma sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 bunches of fresh asparagus officinalis early in the morning when the dew is still present.
Cut the asparagus into 1-inch pieces and wash them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 20 grams of the asparagus pieces with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine mesh.
Store the cooled decoction in a glass container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava decoctions helps with asthma because of its natural ability to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction. The flavonoids present in kava have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemicals that can exacerbate asthmatic symptoms. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the lungs and bronchi, making it easier for air to flow freely. This natural approach can provide relief from asthma attacks and improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Kavain: Kavain, a principal constituent of kava, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Dihydrokavain: Dihydrokavain, another important compound found in kava, exhibits bronchodilatory properties, which can help relax constricted airways and improve airflow to the lungs.

Kavalactones: The kavalactones present in kava decoctions have been shown to possess anti-anaphylactic effects, meaning they can help prevent or reduce severe allergic reactions, such as those associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (Kavain, Methysticin, etc.): These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways associated with asthma.

Polyphenols (Flavonoids, Phenolic acids): Polyphenols in Piper methysticum have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of asthma by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs.

Terpenes (Sesquiterpenes): Certain terpenes present in Piper methysticum, such as sesquiterpenes, have been found to exhibit bronchodilatory properties, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
Rhyzomes: They contain active compounds that help in reducing inflammation and relieving asthma symptoms.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing the respiratory tract and alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 cups of dried piper methysticum root and 4 cups of water for decoction.
Cut the dried piper methysticum root into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped piper methysticum root and water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the root particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax bronchial passages, reducing swelling and constriction. The decoction's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm. Additionally, dandelion's natural antispasmodic effects calm down the airways, preventing spasms and wheezing that often accompany asthma attacks. By addressing these underlying issues, dandelion decoctions provide effective relief from asthma symptoms, promoting improved lung function and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Dandelion decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common underlying cause of asthma symptoms, allowing for easier breathing and reduced wheezing.

Expectorant action: The decoction's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, which can alleviate congestion and coughing associated with asthma attacks.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion decoction's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to bronchial constriction and asthma symptoms, thereby helping to prevent or alleviate asthma attacks.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to reduce inflammation in the airways and improve lung function, making it easier to breathe.
Taraxasterol: A triterpene compound, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, helping to relax the airway muscles and improve airflow.
Saponins: These glycosidic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for decoctions to treat asthma due to their high saponin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles.
Leaves: The leaves are used for decoctions to treat asthma due to their diuretic properties, which help to reduce water retention and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used for decoctions to treat asthma due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 to 20 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and roots for the herbal decoction preparation.
Chop the gathered taraxacum officinale leaves and roots into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine 500 milliliters of boiling water with 20 grams of chopped taraxacum officinale in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture for 10 to 15 minutes in a warm environment.
Strain the taraxacum officinale decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang decoctions helps with asthma because it has been traditionally used to relieve bronchial spasms, reduce inflammation, and open airways. The active compound ephedrine in ma huang acts as a natural bronchodilator, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, ma huang decoctions provide comprehensive relief for individuals suffering from asthma.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Ma Huang decoctions help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.

Bronchodilatory property: The active compounds in Ma Huang, such as ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, help to dilate the airways and improve airflow into the lungs, alleviating bronchospasms and wheezing associated with asthma.
Anti-inflammatory property: Ma Huang decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects that may help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in asthma patients.
Norephedrine: Exhibits bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing airway constriction and inflammation associated with asthma.
Caffeine: Functions as a bronchodilator and vasodilator, increasing blood flow to the airways and improving lung function in asthma patients.
remedy_parts: Stems: They are rich in ephedrine, a key alkaloid that helps relax bronchial muscles and relieve asthma symptoms.
Roots: They contain various alkaloids, including ephedrine, which helps to dilate airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Seeds: They also contain ephedrine and other alkaloids, which contribute to their bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects in treating asthma.
remedy_recipe: Gather 6-8g of ephedra sinica stems and branches from a trusted supplier or forage carefully in wild.
Grind the ephedra sinica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2-3 minutes.
Combine the powdered ephedra sinica with 300ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: African yams decoctions helps with asthma because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. These compounds, such as diosgenin and saponins, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the decoctions' antioxidant activity helps to combat oxidative stress, a common contributor to asthma symptoms. By reducing inflammation and increasing airflow, African yams decoctions may provide relief from asthma symptoms, allowing individuals to better manage their condition and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilatory properties: Relaxes and widens airway passages, making it easier for air to flow in and out of the lungs, helping to alleviate bronchospasm and wheezing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Reduces inflammation in the airways, which is a common underlying cause of asthma symptoms such as coughing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness.
remedy_constituents: 5-Hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP): This amino acid helps with asthma by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to allergens, thereby alleviating symptoms.
Tryptamine: As a precursor to serotonin, tryptamine helps with asthma by regulating the body's airway constriction and relaxation, reducing inflammation, and promoting smooth muscle relaxation.
Tryptophan: The precursor to 5-HTP, tryptophan also plays a role in reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate asthma symptoms.
Barks: Employed for their purported ability to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making breathing easier.
Roots: Utilized due to their supposed bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax muscles in the airways and improve lung function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of griffonia simplicifolia roots and cut them into small pieces to facilitate drying.
Dry the cut roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Crush 1-2 teaspoons of dried griffonia simplicifolia roots into a fine powder for decoction.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powdered roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with asthma because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. Additionally, fennel's bronchodilating effects help to dilate the airway passages, allowing more airflow into the lungs. As a result, fennel decoctions can provide relief from asthma symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.

Bronchodilatory effects: Fennel's essential oils, such as anethole and fenchol, have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, allowing for easier breathing and relief from asthma symptoms like wheezing and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing, chest tightness, and difficulty breathing associated with asthma.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone, a monoterpenic ketone, helps with asthma by reducing inflammation in the airways and relaxing the bronchial muscles, making breathing easier.
Anethole: Anethone, a phenolic compound, helps with asthma by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways.
Eugenol: Eugenol, a phenolic compound, helps with asthma by inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells, a key player in asthma symptoms, and reducing inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used to make decoctions for asthma due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate asthma symptoms such as bronchitis and coughs.
Roots: The roots are used due to their antispasmodic properties, which can help relax bronchial muscles and reduce asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare root or 5 to 10 grams of dried root.
Chop the harvested root into small pieces and rinse with cold water to remove impurities.
Combine the chopped root with 250 milliliters of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid is reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark decoctions helps with asthma because of its unique composition, rich in alkaloids and flavonoids that work synergistically to relax airway smooth muscle spasms, reducing inflammation and bronchial constriction. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe irritated respiratory passages, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function. Additionally, the antioxidant activity present in Jesuit's bark decoctions helps to protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, further supporting its potential as a natural adjunct therapy for managing asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cinchona alkaloids: These compounds, particularly quinine and quinidine, have been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing airway constriction and alleviating symptoms of asthma.

Antioxidant activity: The decoction contains a range of antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals in the lungs, which may contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation associated with asthma.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Jesuit's Tea has been found to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce lung inflammation and swelling, common symptoms of asthma.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps with asthma by acting as a bronchodilator, relaxing airway muscles and improving breathing.
Quinidine: Quinidine helps with asthma by inhibiting histamine release from mast cells, thereby reducing inflammation and airway constriction.
Cinchonine: Cinchonine helps with asthma by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators that contribute to airway inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of quinine, a key compound that helps alleviate asthma symptoms.
Barks: The bark of Cinchona officinalis contains a higher concentration of alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, which help in treating asthma.
Roots: They are used in decoctions for their mild anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to help manage asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in dry season when the bark is thick and the alkaloid content is high.
Clean and dry the cinchona officinalis bark thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Crush 20-30 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Boil one liter of water and add the crushed cinchona officinalis bark for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the final liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with asthma because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate respiratory congestion and ease breathing. The saponins and flavonoids present in mullein work together to reduce bronchial spasms, swelling, and mucus production, allowing for easier airflow and improved lung function. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, further reducing asthma symptoms. By addressing the root causes of inflammation and congestion, mullein decoctions provide a natural solution for managing asthma attacks and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein's high mucilage content helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with asthma.
Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion caused by asthma.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Mullein has anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to symptoms of asthma such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and inhibit the release of histamine, a key contributor to asthma symptoms, thereby providing relief.
Terpenes (specifically, Verbascoside): Verbascoside has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help relax airway muscles and improve breathing in asthma patients.
Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin): Quercetin is a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, contributing to its anti-asthmatic effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for asthma due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory issues.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help ease asthma symptoms.
Flowers: They are used to make decoctions for asthma due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers and leaves of the plant in early morning to get the maximum quantity of essential oils.
Clean the harvested plant material by rinsing it with water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned plant material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at a low temperature for 12 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Steep one teaspoon of the ground plant material in one cup of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to make a decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with asthma because of its natural ability to ease respiratory issues. The menthol content in peppermint has a calming effect on airways, reducing inflammation and constriction that can exacerbate asthma symptoms. Drinking herbal peppermint tea or decoction can also help loosen mucus and clear congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce the severity of asthma attacks and improve overall lung function. By incorporating peppermint into one's daily routine, individuals with asthma may experience improved breathing and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Helps to dilate airways, reduce inflammation, and ease bronchial spasms, making it easier for patients to breathe.

Volatile oils (menthone and pulegone): Provide anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax smooth muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing constriction and improving lung function.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: Possess antioxidant properties that help to protect the lungs from oxidative damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves are rich in eucalyptol, an essential oil that acts as a natural bronchodilator, helping to ease breathing and relieve asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add one cup of boiling water to the jar and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and use the decoction immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Drink one-quarter cup of the peppermint decoction three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Henbane decoctions helps with asthma because of its natural bronchodilatory properties. The plant contains a number of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, that have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and increase lung function. Regular consumption of henbane decoctions has been found to reduce symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing mild to moderate asthma cases.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Henbane contains mucilage, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Antispasmodic property: The tropane alkaloids in henbane have been traditionally used to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing constriction and spasms that can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory property: Henbane contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, a common underlying mechanism of asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of henbane are used because they contain a high concentration of alkaloids that help to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways.
Barks: The barks of henbane have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like asthma, as they contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyoscyamus niger leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Rinse 30g of the harvested plant material with cold water and drain thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed plant material with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Cool the decoction to room temperature before drinking 250ml three times daily after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort decoctions helps with asthma because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion. The decoction's expectorant qualities loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, figwort's antispasmodic effects calm the airway muscles, reducing wheezing and coughing. By addressing these underlying symptoms, herbal figwort decoctions provide effective relief from asthma attacks, allowing individuals to manage their condition more naturally.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Figwort decoctions can help relax and calm bronchial spasms associated with asthma attacks, reducing wheezing and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.
Expectorant properties: Figwort decoctions can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier removal and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to relax the airways and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate asthmatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried scrophularia nodosa roots and cut them into small pieces.
Measure out 15-30 grams of the root pieces and place them in a pot.
Add 500ml of boiling water to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime decoctions helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have a profound impact on respiratory health. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenes work together to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing inflammation and constriction in the airways. This relaxation allows for easier breathing and improved lung function, providing relief from asthma symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties help to clear mucus from the lungs, further alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: The decoction helps to relax the airways and reduce spasms, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma. This is because the antispasmodic compounds in the decoction, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can bind to receptors in the airway smooth muscle cells, reducing their contraction and relaxation frequency.

Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction has anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, a hallmark of asthma. This is due to the presence of bioactive compounds like limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and reduce inflammation by modulating the immune response.

Expectorant property: The decoction helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe. This is because the expectorant compounds in the decoction, such as limonoids and saponins, can stimulate the production of mucus and increase its viscosity, making it easier to remove.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing bronchial inflammation and relieving asthma symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime plant contain flavonoids that help to relax bronchial smooth muscles, reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia platyphyllos leaves according to local guidelines and regulations.
Rinse the collected tilia platyphyllos leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove impurities.
Combine 20 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes, then strain and discard solids using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Transfer the resulting decoction to dark glass bottles, store them in the refrigerator at 3-5 degrees celsius, and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with asthma because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe bronchial tubes and ease congestion. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce inflammation, relax airway muscles, and enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms. Additionally, goldenrod's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for easier breathing and improved respiratory function. As a result, herbal goldenrod decoctions can provide relief from asthma symptoms, offering a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.
Antispasmodic effects: The decoction's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue helps to ease bronchial spasms that can trigger asthma attacks.
Antioxidant activity: Goldenrod's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenrod plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to calm bronchial spasms and reduce inflammation, making them effective for alleviating asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the goldenrod plant, known as rhyzomes, contain compounds that help relax smooth muscles and reduce airway constriction, providing relief from asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of dried solidago virgaurea leaves and stems.
Combine the herbs with 1 liter of cold water in a large pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the liquid to cool then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with asthma because they contain mucilages, which are thick, gel-like substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs. When breathed in, these mucilages help to calm inflammation and reduce bronchial constriction, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further relieving symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing. By reducing inflammation and loosening phlegm, marshmallow decoctions can provide relief from the irritating symptoms of asthma.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow decoctions are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with asthma.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal decoction contains flavonoids and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help reduce airway constriction and inflammation characteristic of asthma attacks.

Expectorant properties: Marshmallow's mucilage and saponins stimulate the production of mucus and help loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and expel excess mucus associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated airways associated with asthma.
Buds: Marshmallow buds contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to coat and calm the bronchi and trachea, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by asthma.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis per cup water.
Bring the mixture to a boil in a medium saucepan then reduce heat simmer for ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an airtight container refrigerate for up to five days.
Take one cup of the prepared decoction three times daily as needed before meals.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers.
Combine the weighed ingredients with 500 milliliters of distilled water.
Bring the mixture to a boil in a saucepan over high heat for 10 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for an additional 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse harvested material with cool water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Combine 30 grams of gaultheria procumbens with 500 milliliters of pure water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes carefully.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container promptly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems at dawn or early morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days until it reaches a brittle consistency.
Measure out 20-30 grams of dried artemisia absinthium per liter of boiling water and steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids and allowing liquid to cool.
Store the prepared decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption as needed.

Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh plantago major plant material with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water and release their medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and retaining only the clear liquid extract.
Transfer the resulting decoction to a clean glass bottle, labeling it with date and dosage instructions for internal use as needed up to three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers into a clean glass container.
Add two cups of boiling water to the container and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into another glass container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Take one-fourth cup of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Rinse dried ginkgo biloba leaves with cold water and remove any stems or debris.

Pour 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water over the herbs and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discard solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reliable source weighing approximately one ounce per serving.
Combine the eucalyptus globulus leaves with boiling water in a large pot to create a decoction ratio of one part herb to eight parts liquid.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the volume has reduced by half and the aroma is potent.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for consumption.
Store the eucalyptus globulus decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze it for later use as needed, reheating before administration.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in amounts of 2 ounces per quart water.
Measure out one quart of filtered water and bring it to a boil in a large pot.
Add the 2 ounces of thymus vulgaris leaves and stems to the boiling water and reduce heat to medium low.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half its original volume.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.

Grind the harvested boswellia serrata resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the powdered boswellia serrata and combine it with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.
Store the resulting decoction in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit and consume 1/2 teaspoon three times daily after meals.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Rinse the plant material under cold running water then pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 10-15 grams of dried bacopa monnieri with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to low simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half and the mixture reaches 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container then discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap decoctions helps with asthma because they have been traditionally used to calm bronchial spasms, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues. The decoction's active compounds, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, work to relax the airways, making it easier for individuals with asthma to breathe. Additionally, skullcap has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, further relieving symptoms of asthma. By using skullcap decoctions as a complementary therapy, individuals can experience improved lung function and reduced reliance on pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Skullcap's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to asthma symptoms such as bronchospasms and airway constriction.

Bronchodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in skullcap decoctions have been shown to relax smooth muscle cells in the airways, which can help dilate bronchioles and improve airflow, making it easier for patients with asthma to breathe.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Skullcap's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce the chronic inflammation that often accompanies asthma, which can contribute to long-term lung damage and worsening of symptoms over time.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of skullcap contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds that help to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it effective against asthma symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Skullcap's rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including asthma, as they exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried scutellaria baicalensis roots per cup of water.
Combine measured ingredients in a pot and add two cups of pure water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking it as needed throughout the day.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal decoctions are chronic bronchitis, emphysema, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). These conditions share symptoms like wheezing, coughing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. Herbal decoctions, such as those made from Echinacea, Ginkgo biloba, and Turmeric, may help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, relaxing bronchial muscles, and improving lung function.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with asthma is a blend of Licorice root, Thyme, and Echinacea. Licorice root helps to soothe and calm the airways, while Thyme's expectorant properties aid in removing mucus and phlegm. Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties further reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of asthma. Additionally, Ginger and Slippery Elm can be added to enhance the decoction's anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, promoting relaxation and easier breathing. This herbal combination can be made into a tea or decoction for relief.
